# Autonomy Phase Source Map

Converted from `126_autonomy_phase_source_map.html` so the pack remains Markdown-only.

```html
<!doctype html>
<html><head><meta charset="utf-8"><title>Autonomy Phase Source Map</title>
<style>body{font-family:Arial, sans-serif; margin:32px; line-height:1.4} table{border-collapse:collapse;width:100%} th,td{border:1px solid #ddd;padding:8px;vertical-align:top} th{background:#f3f3f3}</style>
</head><body>
<h1>Autonomy Phase Source Map</h1>
<p>Official platform/API/policy sources and selected research used for the full autonomous ad factory extension.</p>
<table><thead><tr><th>ID</th><th>Source</th><th>Type</th><th>Use</th><th>Bias note</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td>GOOGLE_AI_MAX_SEARCH_API_2026</td><td><a href='https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/docs/campaigns/ai-max-for-search-campaigns/getting-started'>Google Ads API: AI Max for Search campaigns</a></td><td>Official platform documentation</td><td>Search automation, query expansion, text customisation, URL expansion, brand/message controls, reporting views.</td><td>Platform documentation; accurate for capabilities but naturally pro-platform.</td></tr>
<tr><td>GOOGLE_RSA_API</td><td><a href='https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/docs/responsive-search-ads/create-responsive-search-ads'>Google Ads API: Creating Responsive Search Ads</a></td><td>Official platform documentation</td><td>API payload requirements for responsive search ads: headlines, descriptions, final URLs.</td><td>Platform documentation.</td></tr>
<tr><td>GOOGLE_PMAX_API</td><td><a href='https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/performance-max/create-campaign'>Google Ads API: Create Performance Max Campaign</a></td><td>Official platform documentation</td><td>Performance Max campaign setup, campaign-level brand assets and brand-guideline migration context.</td><td>Platform documentation.</td></tr>
<tr><td>GOOGLE_DEMAND_GEN_API</td><td><a href='https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/docs/demand-gen/overview'>Google Ads API: Demand Gen</a></td><td>Official platform documentation</td><td>Visual surfaces across YouTube, Discover, Gmail; AI-optimised creatives with advertiser-controlled assets.</td><td>Platform documentation.</td></tr>
<tr><td>GOOGLE_EXPERIMENTS_API</td><td><a href='https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/docs/experiments/overview'>Google Ads API: Experiments overview</a></td><td>Official platform documentation</td><td>Campaign experiments, arms, scheduling, promote/graduate/end workflow.</td><td>Platform documentation.</td></tr>
<tr><td>GOOGLE_CONVERSION_LIFT</td><td><a href='https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/12003020?hl=en-AU'>Google Ads Help: About conversion lift</a></td><td>Official platform documentation</td><td>Incrementality and causal measurement; treatment/control lift logic.</td><td>Platform documentation; not a substitute for independent experimental design.</td></tr>
<tr><td>META_MARKETING_API</td><td><a href='https://developers.facebook.com/docs/marketing-api/'>Meta Marketing API</a></td><td>Official platform documentation</td><td>Campaign, ad set, ad creative management through API.</td><td>Platform documentation.</td></tr>
<tr><td>META_LIFT_STUDIES</td><td><a href='https://developers.facebook.com/docs/marketing-api/guides/lift-studies/'>Meta Marketing API: Conversion Lift Measurement</a></td><td>Official platform documentation</td><td>Campaign efficiency and business-impact experiments.</td><td>Platform documentation.</td></tr>
<tr><td>META_AD_STANDARDS</td><td><a href='https://transparency.meta.com/policies/ad-standards/'>Meta Advertising Standards</a></td><td>Official platform policy</td><td>Compliance constraints for deceptive or misleading advertising.</td><td>Platform policy; enforcement details can be opaque.</td></tr>
<tr><td>TIKTOK_API_BUSINESS</td><td><a href='https://ads.tiktok.com/help/article/marketing-api?lang=en'>TikTok API for Business: Marketing API</a></td><td>Official platform documentation</td><td>Batch delivery, creative-material management, intelligent bidding, automated optimisations, audience uploads.</td><td>Platform documentation.</td></tr>
<tr><td>LINKEDIN_CAMPAIGN_API</td><td><a href='https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/linkedin/marketing/integrations/ads/account-structure/create-and-manage-campaigns?view=li-lms-2026-04'>LinkedIn Marketing API: Create and Manage Campaigns</a></td><td>Official platform documentation</td><td>B2B campaign creation, objective types, campaign structure, budgets.</td><td>Platform documentation.</td></tr>
<tr><td>LINKEDIN_CREATIVES_API</td><td><a href='https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/linkedin/marketing/integrations/ads/account-structure/create-and-manage-creatives?view=li-lms-2026-03'>LinkedIn Marketing API: Create and Manage Creatives</a></td><td>Official platform documentation</td><td>Creative limits and format constraints.</td><td>Platform documentation.</td></tr>
<tr><td>OPENAI_AGENTS_SDK</td><td><a href='https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/agents'>OpenAI API: Agents SDK</a></td><td>Official AI developer documentation</td><td>Agents that plan, call tools, coordinate specialist workflows, and keep state across multi-step tasks.</td><td>Vendor documentation; useful for implementation primitives.</td></tr>
<tr><td>OPENAI_STRUCTURED_OUTPUTS</td><td><a href='https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/structured-outputs'>OpenAI API: Structured Outputs</a></td><td>Official AI developer documentation</td><td>Schema-adherent JSON outputs for campaign plans, claims, tests, compliance reports, payload drafts.</td><td>Vendor documentation.</td></tr>
<tr><td>OPENAI_FUNCTION_CALLING</td><td><a href='https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/function-calling'>OpenAI API: Function calling</a></td><td>Official AI developer documentation</td><td>Tool calls to databases, ad APIs, approval queues, analytics warehouses, and validators.</td><td>Vendor documentation.</td></tr>
<tr><td>OPENAI_IMAGE_API</td><td><a href='https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/image-generation'>OpenAI API: Image generation</a></td><td>Official AI developer documentation</td><td>Image generation/editing for static creative concepts and variations.</td><td>Vendor documentation; outputs still require rights, brand, and policy review.</td></tr>
<tr><td>OPENAI_AGENT_GUIDE</td><td><a href='https://openai.com/business/guides-and-resources/a-practical-guide-to-building-ai-agents/'>OpenAI: Practical guide to building AI agents</a></td><td>Vendor implementation guide</td><td>Single-agent vs multi-agent patterns, tools, guardrails, evals, model/cost optimisation.</td><td>Vendor guide; useful implementation framework.</td></tr>
<tr><td>GOOGLE_MISREPRESENTATION_POLICY</td><td><a href='https://support.google.com/adspolicy/answer/6020955?hl=en'>Google Ads Policy: Misrepresentation</a></td><td>Official platform policy</td><td>Clear/honest ads, avoiding misleading product/service/business information, unreliable claims, clickbait, unclear relevance.</td><td>Platform policy; enforcement can be conservative.</td></tr>
<tr><td>ACCC_FALSE_MISLEADING</td><td><a href='https://www.accc.gov.au/consumers/advertising-and-promotions/false-or-misleading-claims'>ACCC: False or misleading claims</a></td><td>Australian regulator guidance</td><td>Claims must be true, accurate, based on reasonable grounds, and provable.</td><td>Regulator guidance; jurisdiction is Australia.</td></tr>
<tr><td>FTC_AI_ADS</td><td><a href='https://www.ftc.gov/industry/technology/artificial-intelligence'>FTC: Artificial Intelligence</a></td><td>US regulator hub</td><td>AI advertising claims, deceptive AI practices, substantiation expectations.</td><td>Regulator guidance; jurisdiction is US.</td></tr>
<tr><td>BANDIT_ADS_JD</td><td><a href='https://cdn.aaai.org/ojs/17775/17775-13-21269-1-2-20210518.pdf'>Comparison Lift: Bandit-based Experimentation System for Online Advertising</a></td><td>Academic/industry research paper</td><td>Bandit-based online ad experimentation architecture and exploration/exploitation tradeoffs.</td><td>Single platform/case; useful for architecture, not universal proof.</td></tr>
<tr><td>FACEBOOK_INCREMENTALITY_DESIGN</td><td><a href='https://arxiv.org/abs/1806.02588'>Designing Experiments to Measure Incrementality on Facebook</a></td><td>Academic paper</td><td>Power and sample-size issues in lift-style ad incrementality experiments.</td><td>Methodological; not a modern platform manual.</td></tr>
<tr><td>GOOGLE_DEMAND_GEN_DROP_2026</td><td><a href='https://blog.google/products/ads-commerce/demand-gen-drop-march-2026/'>Google Ads & Commerce Blog: Demand Gen Drop March 2026</a></td><td>Official platform blog</td><td>Veo in Google Ads, creative asset variation, creator partnerships.</td><td>Marketing blog; useful for direction of platform automation.</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
</body></html>
```
